rationale: Title Y is stronger overall because it adds the management focus and the patient's exact age and sex, which makes the case more specific and clinically relevant for PACE. Title X is cleaner and more concise, but it undersells the distinctive contribution of the report.
loser_weakness: Title X is clear, but comparatively generic. It names the complications and procedure, yet omits the individualized antithrombotic management angle that likely makes the case publishable.
